description_long: This is a rectangular metal wire fence panel composed of evenly spaced vertical rods intersected by three horizontal rails. The structure is open and grid-like, allowing visibility and airflow through its large rectangular gaps. The top and bottom edges are straight, with no decorative elements or post caps, giving it a utilitarian and minimalist appearance. The metal rods are thin and uniformly distributed, suggesting the fence is lightweight and portable, suitable for temporary or semi-permanent installation. The finish appears to be a dark, possibly powder-coated or painted metal, providing some resistance to weathering. This type of fence is commonly used in urban environments for crowd control, property demarcation, or as a barrier in parks and public spaces.
description: A minimalist rectangular metal wire fence panel with evenly spaced vertical rods and three horizontal rails, finished in a dark color. The design is open, utilitarian, and suitable for urban barriers or property demarcation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full grid of vertical rods and three horizontal rails, with a flat top and bottom edge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with rods appearing as a single line.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually identical to the front, displaying the same grid pattern and structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge profile of the fence.
